structured.title: Family Trip for Chips and Cardiff Match Commentary
structured.overview: A family drives out to pick up chips from Irene’s on the way to watch a Cardiff City football match together. In the car they try to tune BBC Radio Wales to the Cardiff-region FM frequency to catch live commentary of the game, discussing frequencies and reception. At the chip shop they order chips, saveloy sausage, fish cake, and mild curry sauce and later complain that the chips are undercooked and taste old. Back at home, they join other family members, juggle seating (including someone lying on the bed), greet the dog Bruce, and listen intently to live match commentary. They talk about the poor state of the Doncaster pitch, rugby league damage, Cardiff’s form since December, individual players like Willock, Rubin and Robinson, tactical issues, and refereeing decisions. There is side chat about WiFi routers (upgrading to WiFi 7 via the ISP), Disney trips, local chip shops in Cardiff, and a children’s arcade trip, while everyone follows the match and shares reactions in a relaxed family setting.

apps_results.content:
## Pre-match and travel
- Family driving to football match, worried about being late  
- Trying to tune BBC Radio Wales: 93–104 FM, Cardiff 104.9  
- Decide to stop at Irene’s for chips and curry sauce  

## Food stop and family logistics
- Order: small chips, saveloy sausage, fish cake, mild curry  
- Call home to say arriving in 5–10 minutes  
- Plan to blame “Eddie was hungry” for buying chips  
- Concern about mum’s reaction to buying takeaway  

## Arrival home and chip review
- Chips described as “barely cooked” and “anemic”  
- Very small portion; food tastes old, not fresh  
- Decision not to go to Irene’s again for chips  

## Watching the Cardiff match
- Listening to Cardiff vs Doncaster on BBC Radio Wales  
- Pitch described as “diabolical,” shared rugby league surface  
- Commentators: Cardiff like to play on the floor but can’t  
- Cardiff score via good assist from “Laura/Lola,” praise for Robertson  

## Match analysis and other teams
- Discussion of Willock: good attack, weak tracking back  
- Talk about Chambers and Osho, defensive blame from last week  
- Mentions of Newport, Lincoln, Stevenage, Stockport results  
- Crowd about 700; called a “six-pointer” game  

## Home life and tech
- Dog Bruce mentioned, kids moving between sofa and bed  
- WiFi upgrade: new WiFi 7 router from provider  
- QR code used for easy guest WiFi access  

## Next steps
- Avoid Irene’s in future; find better chip shop  
- Continue following Cardiff’s season despite stress  
- Use new router/QR setup for visitors’ devices
